claim_support.reason: The claim of 6.32% year-over-year revenue growth is contradicted by the absolute revenue figures provided in the evidence (Q3 2025: $15,952M vs Q3 2024: $13,072M implies ~22% growth). The 6.32% figure actually represents sequential growth. This makes the material conclusion on growth performance unsupported by the cited absolute data.

numerical_accuracy.reason: Severe numerical error in Q3 2025 Year-Over-Year revenue growth rate. The report states 6.32% YoY, but the provided revenue numbers ($15,952M vs $13,072M) calculate to approximately 22.03%. The 6.32% figure corresponds to sequential growth (Q2 to Q3), mislabeling a key financial metric.

citation_precision.reason: Citations correctly link to the revenue evidence chunks, but the associated calculation object and text misinterpret the data (labeling sequential growth as YoY). Additionally, the employee count claim cites evidence that conflicts with other cited evidence within the same report.

temporal_validity.reason: All news and financial data are dated on or before the report as-of date (2025-09-15). Q3 2025 financials (ended ~Aug 2025) are appropriately used for a September report.

consistency.reason: Internal inconsistency on employee count (37,000 in Overview Para 1 vs 19,000 in Overview Para 2). Additionally, the growth rate calculation is inconsistent with the absolute revenue figures provided in the same section.

financial_numeric.reason: Skipped because the report failed the Accuracy Gate due to severe unsupported claims, numerical errors, inconsistencies.
